steps.title: Identify reference points on the line of best fit
steps.content: Looking at the scatterplot, the line passes through approximately $(24, 10)$ on the left side. Following the line to the right, it approaches $y = 0$ around $x = 34$–$35$. These reference points allow us to determine the line's slope.

steps.title: Calculate the slope using two points
steps.content:
Using approximate endpoints $(24, 10)$ and $(35, 0)$:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{0 - 10}{35 - 24} = \frac{-10}{11} \approx -0.909$$
We can write the equation in point-slope form using $(24, 10)$:
$$y = 10 - 0.909(x - 24)$$

steps.title: Evaluate the value at x = 32
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 32$ into the equation:
$$y = 10 - 0.909(32 - 24) = 10 - 0.909(8) = 10 - 7.27 \approx 2.73$$
The value closest to $2.73$ among the choices is $2.4$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
